Package org.eventb.internal.core.seqprover.eventbExtensions.tactics

Class FunAppInDomGoalTac

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.core.seqprover.eventbExtensions.tactics.FunAppInDomGoalTac
All Implemented Interfaces:
ITactic
public class FunAppInDomGoalTac extends Object implements ITactic
If the goal is such as f(x)∈dom(g) :
  • First, we try to add hypothesis such as f(x)∈E using the FunImageGoal reasoner.
  • If it succeeds, we try to re-write dom(g) using TotalDomRewrites.
  • Finally, if it succeeds, we try to discharge the goal using MembershipGoal. If the goal is discharged, then the tactics are applied.
These tests are done for every possible case of re-writing dom(g) and every possible added hypothesis.

  • Method Details

    • apply

      public Object apply(IProofTreeNode ptNode, IProofMonitor pm)
      Description copied from interface: ITactic
      Applies this tactic to a proof tree at a specific node.

      The convention is that a tactic applied at a particular node is allowed to modify only the subtree rooted at that node, but this is not checked.

      Specified by:
      apply in interface ITactic
      Parameters:
      ptNode - The proof tree node at which this tactic should be applied
      pm - The proof monitor to monitor the progress of the tactic
      Returns:
      null iff the application was successfull.

      Note : The current convention for the return type is that in case the tactic was not successful, the proof tree is not modified, and the reason for tactic failure can be read from the toString() method from the object returned. In the near future the return type for the tactic will be refined to return more information useful for composing tactics. Implementors should therefore not rely on the return type at this time.

      See Also:
      • IProofMonitor

    • createSets

      private void createSets(Expression f, Set<Predicate> set_f_AopB, Expression g, Set<Expression> setDomain_g, Predicate hypothesis)
      If the predicate hypothesis matches f∈A op B (with "op" an function or a relation, and f is the given expression), then the predicate is added to the set set_f_AopB.

      Else, if the predicate hypothesis matches g∈A op B (with "op" a total function or a total relation, and g is the given expression), then the expression A is added to the set setDomain_g.

      Else, nothing happens.

      Parameters:
      f - the function or relation extracted from the goal matching f(x) ∈ dom(g)
      set_f_AopB - the set containing all hypotheses matching f∈ A op B ("f" is given).
      g - the total function or total relation extracted from the goal matching f(x) ∈ dom(g)
      setDomain_g - the set containing all expressions "A" extracted from hypotheses matching g ∈ A op B.
      hypothesis - the tested predicate (should be a hypothesis of the sequent).

    • isFunOrRel

      private boolean isFunOrRel(Expression expression)
      Return true iff the expression expression is a function or a relation
      Parameters:
      expression - the considered expression
      Returns:
      true iff the expression expression is a function or a relation

    • isTFunOrTRel

      private boolean isTFunOrTRel(Expression expression)
      Return true iff the expression expression is a total function or a total relation
      Parameters:
      expression - the considered expression
      Returns:
      true iff the expression expression is a total function or a total relation

    • applyFunImageGoal

      private IProofTreeNode applyFunImageGoal(Predicate f_AopB, IProofTreeNode ptNode, IProofMonitor pm)
      Try to apply the reasoner FunImageGoal on the given IProofTreeNode ptNode with the IProofManager pm . The predicate f_AopB (should be a hypothesis matching f∈ A op B ("op" either a relation or a function)) is used in the input of the reasoner.

      It returns null if the reasoner failed or if it created more than one sub-goal, it returns the proof tree node child else.

      Parameters:
      f_AopB - the predicate used for the input of the reasoner
      ptNode - the considered IProofTreeNode
      pm - the considered IProofMonitor
      Returns:
      null if the reasoner failed or if it created more than one sub-goal, else it returns the resulting proof tree node.

    • applyTotalDomRewrites

      private IProofTreeNode applyTotalDomRewrites(Expression domain_g, IProofTreeNode ptNode, IProofMonitor pm)
      Try to apply the reasoner TotalDomRewrites on the given IProofTreeNode ptNode with the IProofManager pm . The expression domain_g (should be a set "A" matching a hypothesis g∈ A op B (with "g" given)) is used in the input of the reasoner.
      Parameters:
      domain_g - the expression (should be a set) used for the input of the reasoner
      ptNode - the considered IProofTreeNode
      pm - the considered IProofMonitor
      Returns:
      null if the reasoner failed or if it created more than one sub-goal, else it returns the resulting proof tree node.

    • preCompute

      private Object preCompute(IProofTreeNode ptNode, Set<Predicate> set_f_AopB, Set<Expression> setDomain_g, IProofMonitor pm)
      Test if the sequent's goal of the given IProofTreeNode ptNode matches f(x)∈dom(g). If so, in the set set_f_AopB will be added every hypotheses matching f ∈ A op B (with "op" etiher a function or a relation); and in the set setDomain_g will be added every expression "A" matching g ∈ A op B (with "op" either a total relation or a total function). If these two sets are non-empty, then it return null.
      Parameters:
      ptNode - the considered IProofTreeNode
      set_f_AopB - a set of every hypothesis matching f ∈ A op B (with "op" either a relation or a function) (should be empty)
      setDomain_g - a set of every expression "A" matching g ∈ A op B (with "op" either a total relation or a total function) (should be empty)
      pm - support cancellation (must not be null
      Returns:
      null iff the sequent's goal of the given IProofTreeNode ptNode matches f(x)∈dom(g) and set_f_AopB and setDomain_g are non empty in the end.

    • finalCondition

      private boolean finalCondition(IProofTreeNode ptNode)
      Returns whether the given node has any pending descendant (i.e., is either open or closed).
      Parameters:
      ptNode - the considered proof tree node
      Returns:
      true iff the given node has no pending descendant
